phase_1.audio: Sound level monitoring shows gradual decline from 48.7 dBA to sustained 35.7 dBA. Overall environment is exceptionally quiet, highly conducive to concentration.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 82 bpm throughout session (moderately elevated, consistent with active cognitive engagement). Data indicates user was in suboptimal physiological state for concentration.

phase_2.correlations: Correlation analysis reveals moderate compatibility between positional, kinetic, and acoustic factors.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session shows moderate concentration quality with mixed environmental factors. Acoustic baseline of 35.7 dB provides acceptable conditions. User maintained variable positioning.

phase_3.recommendations: Establish dedicated seating to minimize postural shifts and maintain focus.

reason: Concentration score reflects moderate audio conditions (35.7dB), frequent motion, and adequate session length.
